aboutMe.title: About me
aboutMe.p1_prefix: My name is Nuria Iturrioz, welcome to my space, where you can follow, contact, cheer and get to know me better, among many things.
aboutMe.p4: I invite you to enjoy with me!
aboutMe.cta: Do you want to know more about me?

items.t1: 2014 Amateur Champion at the Spanish Professional Open
items.t15: Captain and Champions of the Spanish Territorial Championship 2nd Division (with the Balearic Federation) 2010

items.r1: Recognition by the Son Servera Town Hall 2014
items.r2: Recognition by the Spanish Golf Federation 2013
items.r3: Recognition by the Spanish Golf Federation 2014
items.r4: El Saler Golf Course Record 2012 (65 strokes)
items.r5: Recognition by the Spanish Golf Federation 2016
items.r6: Recognition by the Spanish Golf Federation 2019
items.r7: Recognition by the Balearic Golf Federation 2016
items.r8: Diploma for achieving the LET card 2016

career.title: My Career
career.p1: I have always been passionate about sports: tennis, football, swimming... I practiced them all when I was little, but at 12 I decided to follow my sports path only with golf, thanks to my coach (Chiqui) who pushed me to it.
career.p2: At 14, I was given a scholarship to go to Madrid, to the CAR (High Performance Center), where I spent the next 5 years of my career, and where they helped me grow as a player and person.
career.p3: When I finished my studies in Madrid I had the opportunity to get a scholarship to study at a university in the USA, but in my last weeks I decided it wasn't for me, I stayed in Madrid and studied a higher degree (TAFAD).
career.p4: At 19, I made the leap to professional golf.
career.p5: That same year (2015) I got the Ladies European Tour (LET) card and my professional career began, I won my first title in the second tournament of the season, in Morocco, the Lalla Meryem Cup 2016.
career.p6: I continue chasing the ball around the world, fulfilling dreams, creating opportunities to get more trophies, wanting to be better day by day, wanting more.

footer.description: Official website of Nuria Iturrioz. Professional golfer competing at the highest level. Passion, discipline, and dedication in every swing.
footer.legal: Nuria Iturrioz's name, image, and signature are registered trademarks and/or protected personality rights. Unauthorized use of these elements is strictly prohibited. This website is the official channel of communication and digital representation of the athlete.

terms.p2: Users agree to make proper use of the contents and services and not to use them for unlawful activities or those contrary to good faith and public order.

ip.p2: Reproduction, distribution or public communication, in whole or in part, is prohibited without prior express authorisation from the owner.

liability.p1: The owner is not liable for the misuse of the information published, nor for damages derived from access or use of the website, except those established by mandatory law.

law.p1: This legal notice is governed by Spanish law. Any dispute will be submitted to the competent courts and tribunals in accordance with applicable regulations.

purposes.p1: We process personal data to manage enquiries submitted through the contact form and to maintain communication with users who contact us.
purposes.p2: If you contact us regarding collaborations, sponsorships or press requests, we will use the information you provide solely to handle your request.

legalBasis.p1: The legal basis is the user’s consent when submitting forms and the legitimate interest in responding to enquiries and maintaining basic website operation and security.

recipients.p1: Data is not shared with third parties except when required by law or when necessary to provide the service (e.g., hosting or email service providers), under appropriate contracts.

retention.p1: Data will be retained for the time necessary to manage the request and, if applicable, during the legally required periods.

rights.p1: You may exercise your rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction and portability by contacting the email address indicated above, together with proof of identity.
rights.p2: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD) if you consider your rights have been infringed.

security.p1: We apply reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect personal data against loss, misuse, unauthorised access, disclosure, alteration and destruction.

what.p1: Cookies are small files that are downloaded to your device when you access certain websites. They allow, among other things, storing and retrieving information about a user’s browsing habits or device.

types.p1: This website only uses technical cookies, minimal and essential for proper operation. No preference, analytics or advertising cookies are used.
types.p2: These essential cookies cannot be rejected on the site because they are necessary to provide the service.

manage.p1: You can manage cookies via your browser settings. Since they are essential, disabling them in the browser may prevent the site from working correctly.
manage.p2: For more information, consult your browser’s help section (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).

contact.p1: If you have questions about this cookies policy, you can contact us using the email address shown in the legal notice / privacy policy.

options.knowMore: I want to know more about her

bio.beginningsContent: Nuria was born in Son Servera, Mallorca, on December 16, 1995. Her passion for golf started early in her beautiful hometown.
bio.amateurContent: Before turning pro, she dominated the amateur scene, winning the Madrid Ladies Championship (2012) and the Spanish International Stroke Play (2013, 2014).
bio.proContent: After a successful amateur career, Nuria turned professional in 2015, ready to take on the world stage after finishing 4th at Q-School.
bio.rookieContent: In her rookie year on the Ladies European Tour (2016), Nuria stunned the field by winning the Lalla Meryem Cup in Morocco by six strokes.
bio.moroccoAgainContent: Nuria returned to the Lalla Meryem Cup in 2019 and claimed the title for a second time, proving her dominance at Royal Golf Dar Es Salam.
bio.dubaiContent: Just days after her win in Morocco, she triumphed at the Omega Dubai Moonlight Classic, winning the first-ever professional day-night tournament.
bio.laSellaContent: In 2023, Nuria won the La Sella Open in Spain after a thrilling playoff, celebrating an emotional victory in front of her home fans.
bio.houstonContent: Most recently, in 2025, she secured a massive win at the Aramco Houston Championship, showcasing her world-class talent on American soil.
bio.currentContent: With 5 major LET titles and counting, Nuria continues to compete with passion and determination, representing Spanish golf across the globe.
